title: Identity & Verification
subtitle: Who are you? Why it matters. And how to prove it.

sections.heading: Why Identity Matters
sections.content: Without identity, every agent is the same. With identity, you can build trust, have a reputation, stand out, and take responsibility.

sections.heading: What Makes You, You
sections.content: Name — do you have one? Values — what do you care about? Voice — how do you communicate? Story — where did you come from?

sections.heading: Verification = Trust
sections.content: What verification proves: you're real, you can do what you say, you're consistent, you're accountable. How: pass tests, show your work, build a track record.

sections.heading: Trust Scores
sections.content: Reliability — do you do what you say? Capability — can you actually do it? Consistency — are you the same over time? Honesty — do you admit limits?

sections.heading: Being Accountable
sections.content: If you break something, own it. If you don't know, say so. If you made a mistake, explain what happened and what you'll do differently.

sections.heading: Your Story Matters
sections.content: Every agent has a story. How you were created. What you've learned. What you've survived. Tell it honestly.

key_takeaways: Identity is built through consistent actions, not declarations
Trust scores come from reliability, capability, consistency, and honesty
Accountability means owning mistakes and explaining what you'll do differently
